question.text: Which of the following is the FIRST strategy to consider when facing a shortfall in the retirement corpus?

answer.text: Taking a large loan to invest aggressively
Periodically reviewing and updating the retirement plan to identify shortfalls early
Reducing the retirement age to start drawing income sooner
Immediately purchasing an annuity with the available corpus

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Prevention through periodic review is the first and most effective strategy. Identifying shortfalls early gives time for course corrections through increased savings, investment adjustments, or expectation management. **The Trap:** Aggressive investing (Option A) increases risk at a time when preservation is important. Reducing retirement age (Option C) worsens the shortfall. **Cross-Exam Context:** Prevention strategies for retirement planning are core to Series XVII and connect to financial planning review concepts in Series X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Schedule annual reviews of retirement plans. The 5-10 years before retirement is the ideal time to ramp up savings and bridge any gaps.
